title.en: Payback period: evaluating a $50M marketing-channel investment
title.es: Período de recuperación: evaluar una inversión de $50M en canal de marketing

question.en: Capital One is evaluating a $50M investment in a new marketing channel. Leadership has set a hard requirement: payback must be achieved within 3 years. What incremental annual cash flow would the channel need to generate to meet that threshold? What assumptions does that number depend on, and what would make those assumptions fragile?
question.es: Capital One está evaluando una inversión de $50M en un nuevo canal de marketing. La dirección ha establecido un requisito estricto: la recuperación debe lograrse en 3 años. ¿Qué flujo de caja anual incremental necesitaría generar el canal para cumplir ese umbral? ¿De qué suposiciones depende ese número, y qué haría que esas suposiciones fueran frágiles?

must_include: the explicit payback calculation: required annual cash flow = $50M / 3 years ≈ $16.7M per year

a named definition of 'incremental': the $16.7M must be net of variable costs the channel incurs — not gross revenue from new customers, but contribution margin after channel-specific costs (creative, ops, servicing of acquired customers)

acknowledgment of the limitation of payback as a metric: it ignores cash flows beyond year 3 and the time value of money — a channel that pays back in year 3 then generates zero is very different from one that pays back in year 3 and continues generating $10M/year for a decade

at least two specific fragility factors — e.g., customer LTV assumption (churn rate changes the cash flow curve), channel efficiency degradation (cost-per-acquisition rising as easy audiences are exhausted), attribution model uncertainty (how much revenue is truly incremental vs would have come anyway)

a complementary NPV framing — noting that a ≤3-year payback at $16.7M/year is a much weaker signal than the NPV at the firm's cost of capital, which is the fuller question management should also be asking
must_avoid: confusing gross revenue with incremental cash flow — new customers acquired through the channel carry variable costs that must be subtracted before the payback calculation is meaningful
treating the 3-year payback requirement as the complete investment criterion without noting what it does not measure
point-estimate confidence without naming the assumptions that could cause the actual payback to be 4 or 5 years instead of 3
